title_zh: NVIDIA Nemotron 在 LangChain 深度代理集成下实现基准领先性能
title_en: NVIDIA Nemotron Achieves Benchmark-Leading Performance With LangChain Deep Agents Harness

summary_en: NVIDIA Nemotron 3 Ultra delivers leading performance at a lower cost than top closed models by leveraging the largest and most widely adopted AI agent orchestration platform. LangChain has fine-tuned its Deep Agents harness for NVIDIA Nemotron 3 Ultra, achieving the highest accuracy among open models, completing more tasks with higher throughput, and running at 10x speed.
why_it_matters_zh: 这一合作显著提升了开放模型的性能和效率，降低成本，同时推动AI代理技术的广泛应用。
why_it_matters_en: This collaboration significantly enhances performance and efficiency of open models at lower costs, advancing the adoption of AI agent technologies.

title_zh: AI创新者采用NVIDIA Vera——为何规模化最大单线程CPU至关重要
title_en: AI Innovators Adopt NVIDIA Vera — Why Max Single-Threaded CPU at Scale Matters
summary_zh: 最大单线程CPU在规模化部署中是为具备自主能力的AI时代专门打造的新型CPU类别。CPU在自主系统的创建与部署过程中至关重要，负责推理、响应时间和学习。CPU执行AI模型指令，包括工具调用和代码执行等关键任务。
summary_en: Max single-threaded CPUs at scale represent a new category of CPUs designed for the agentic AI era. Throughout the creation and deployment of agentic systems, the CPU is critical for reasoning, response time, and learning. CPUs execute the work commanded by the AI model, including tool calling and code execution.
why_it_matters_zh: 随着AI系统朝自主智能方向发展，CPU在推理和实时响应中的性能成为瓶颈。NVIDIA Vera的出现优化了单线程处理能力，使AI模型能更高效地执行指令，提升整体系统表现。
why_it_matters_en: As AI systems become more agentic, CPU performance in reasoning and real-time response is a bottleneck. NVIDIA Vera enhances single-threaded processing power, enabling AI models to execute instructions more efficiently and improving overall system performance.

title_zh: CSTutorBench：作为导师评估小型语言模型在基于积木编程中的表现
title_en: CSTutorBench: Benchmarking Small Language Models as Tutors for Block-Based Programming
summary_zh: 大语言模型作为AI导师在K-12教育中应用越来越多，但隐私、成本和对专有模型的依赖引发担忧。小型语言模型（SLM）提供了有希望的替代方案，但针对特定教育场景（如基于积木的编程）选择合适模型依然困难。本文提出了CSTutorBench基准，用于评估语言模型在VEX VR机器人环境中担任计算机科学导师的能力。该基准包含基于17个场景的问题，采用融合教学和反馈研究的教学评分标准，并通过人机结合的LLM评分管线进行评估。对11个参数规模在4B到120B间模型的初步测试表明，模型在词汇和语气等表层指标表现良好，但在避免答案泄露和利用学生调试历史等深层教学行为上表现不足。结果显示，模型家族和指令微调方法较参数数量更能预测教学质量，经过针对性提示词优化后，10个模型的表现有所提升。这一结果强调了基于具体教育情境和教学理论的基准对SLM教育部署选择的重要性。
summary_en: Large language models are increasingly used as AI tutors in K-12 education, but concerns exist regarding privacy, cost, and reliance on proprietary models. Small language models (SLMs) offer a promising alternative, yet selecting the right model for specific educational contexts—such as block-based programming—is challenging due to limited domain data. This paper introduces CSTutorBench, a benchmark to evaluate language models as computer science tutors within the VEX VR block-based robotics environment. The benchmark features 17 scenario-based questions scored with a pedagogical rubric grounded in tutoring and feedback research, assessed via a human-in-the-loop LLM judging pipeline. Preliminary testing across 11 models (4B-120B parameters) shows models perform well on surface-level criteria like vocabulary and tone but struggle with deeper pedagogical behaviors, including avoiding answer leakage and engaging with student debugging histories. Model family and instruction tuning are better quality predictors than size alone. Targeted prompt revisions improved 10 of 11 models. These results highlight the value of context-specific, pedagogically grounded benchmarks for selecting SLMs in educational deployments.
why_it_matters_zh: 该研究聚焦于教育领域中小型语言模型的有效选用，特别是在基于积木的编程教学环境中，填补了相关基准缺失的空白。通过结合人类反馈和教育理论的评分体系，研究明确了模型在真实教学场景下的优劣，指导了未来教育AI产品的开发和优化。
why_it_matters_en: This research addresses the challenge of effectively selecting small language models for education, especially in block-based programming contexts where benchmarks are scarce. By integrating human feedback and pedagogical theory into the evaluation, it exposes models’ strengths and weaknesses in real teaching tasks, guiding future development and optimization of AI tutors for education.

ranking_reason: Selected from authoritative primary source; quality score 84.

title_zh: SearchEyes：通过搜索世界模拟迈向前沿多模态深度搜索智能
title_en: SearchEyes: Towards Frontier Multimodal Deep Search Intelligence via Search World Simulation
summary_zh: 本文提出了SearchEyes，一种以类型化知识图为核心的模拟搜索世界，该世界统一构建训练数据、搜索环境和奖励信号，解决多跳推理训练中结构断连的问题。通过引入感知-知识链（PKC）和跃点锚定策略优化（HaPO），实现了多模态搜索代理的高效训练。实验表明，SearchEyes在六个多模态知识密集基准上表现优异，SearchEyes-27B版本平均超越现有最强开源模型6.2分。
summary_en: This paper introduces SearchEyes, which uses a typed knowledge graph as the backbone of a simulated search world that unifies training data, environments, and rewards to address structural disconnects in multi-hop reasoning training. It proposes Perception-Knowledge Chains (PKC) and Hop-Anchored Policy Optimization (HaPO) to enable efficient learning of multimodal search agents. Experiments on six knowledge-intensive benchmarks demonstrate that SearchEyes achieves state-of-the-art results, with the SearchEyes-27B model outperforming the strongest open-source baseline by 6.2 points on average.
why_it_matters_zh: SearchEyes通过统一构建训练机制和利用结构化知识图，精准捕获多跳推理的细节及奖励分配，显著提升了多模态搜索智能的性能，推动了该领域的发展。
why_it_matters_en: By unifying training mechanisms and leveraging structured knowledge graphs to capture multi-hop reasoning details and reward assignment accurately, SearchEyes significantly improves multimodal search intelligence performance, advancing the field.
image_prompt: Modern editorial square illustration for an AI news app. Topic: SearchEyes: Towards Frontier Multimodal Deep Search Intelligence via Search World Simulation. Category: image_ai. Clean, premium, technology-focused, no text, no logos.
ranking_reason: Selected from authoritative primary source; quality score 75.

title_zh: 注入还是导航？面向大语言模型分析交易法律文件的高效令牌检索
title_en: Inject or Navigate? Token-Efficient Retrieval for LLM Analysis of Transactional Legal Documents
summary_zh: 本文研究了在交易法律文件问答中，替代将整个语料库注入大语言模型上下文窗口的两种结构化检索方式：嵌入检索（NAVEMBED）和基于紧凑结构化索引的大语言模型导航（NAVINDEX）。实验表明，NAVINDEX在保证准确率的同时，显著减少了输入令牌数量、缩减了语境长度并降低了成本。
summary_en: This paper investigates replacing full-corpus injection into large language model context windows for question answering over transactional legal documents with two structured retrieval methods: embedding retrieval (NAVEMBED) and LLM navigation over a compact structured index (NAVINDEX). Results show NAVINDEX achieves comparable accuracy while substantially reducing input tokens, context size, and cost.
why_it_matters_zh: 传统全文注入方法令牌成本高且上下文受限，本文方法通过巧妙结构化检索显著提升了令牌效率和系统性能，降低了计算资源和费用，对法律文档自动分析具有重要意义。
why_it_matters_en: Traditional full injection methods incur high token costs and suffer from long-context degradation. This work's structured retrieval approaches greatly improve token efficiency and system performance, reducing computational resources and costs, which is significant for automated legal document analysis.
image_prompt: Modern editorial square illustration for an AI news app. Topic: Inject or Navigate? Token-Efficient Retrieval for LLM Analysis of Transactional Legal Documents. Category: research_papers. Clean, premium, technology-focused, no text, no logos.
ranking_reason: Selected from authoritative primary source; quality score 65.

title_zh: 通过混合模式优势正则化减轻大型推理模型中的事实幻觉问题
title_en: Mitigating Factual Hallucination in Large Reasoning Models via Mixed-Mode Advantage Regularization
summary_zh: 大型推理模型通过生成显式思维路径提升语言模型能力，尤其在事实导向的问答中帮助恢复相关知识和优化答案。不过，显式思维有时会推翻原本正确的非思维答案，引发所谓的“思维诱发幻觉”，导致事实错误。该研究提出将显式思维视为模型直接回答倾向上的一个思维残差，可能带来缺失知识的补充或引入无支持的关联。基于此，提出了MARGO——一种利用非思维路径作为同模型参考的强化学习框架，通过混合思维和非思维的轨迹组评估显式思维是否增加事实价值，从而抑制幻觉风险同时保持思维的正面作用。多基准实验证明，MARGO在提升事实可靠性方面优于强基线，且在数学推理中保持了良好的推理能力。
summary_en: Large reasoning models enhance language models by generating explicit thinking traces before final answers, which helps recover knowledge and refine answers in factual question answering (QA). However, explicit thinking can sometimes overturn correct non-thinking answers, causing "thinking-induced hallucination." This work models explicit thinking as a residual over the model’s direct-answer tendency that can either add missing knowledge or introduce unsupported associations. To address this, the authors propose MARGO, a reinforcement learning framework that uses mixed-mode rollouts—both thinking and non-thinking trajectories—to evaluate if explicit thinking adds factual value beyond direct answering. By suppressing hallucination-prone thinking while preserving beneficial thinking behaviors, MARGO improves factual reliability on multiple QA benchmarks and maintains general reasoning ability on mathematical tasks.
why_it_matters_zh: 这一研究针对大型推理模型在事实问答中出现的思维诱发幻觉问题提出创新解决方案，通过混合模式优势正则化，巧妙利用同模型的非思维轨迹作为参考，避免了显式思维带来的错误偏差。这提升了模型的事实准确性，同时保持了其推理能力，对推动事实导向的自然语言处理模型发展具有重要意义。
why_it_matters_en: This research addresses the challenge of thinking-induced hallucination in large reasoning models for factual QA by introducing a novel mixed-mode advantage regularization approach. By leveraging same-model non-thinking trajectories as references, it effectively suppresses factual errors introduced by explicit thinking while retaining beneficial reasoning. This advancement enhances the factual reliability and robustness of language models, which is critical for trustworthy AI applications in natural language understanding and reasoning.
image_prompt: Modern editorial square illustration for an AI news app. Topic: Mitigating Factual Hallucination in Large Reasoning Models via Mixed-Mode Advantage Regularization. Category: research_papers. Clean, premium, technology-focused, no text, no logos.
